row1.revenue: Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) takes the lead with a revenue of 716.9B USD, narrowly surpassing Walmart Inc. (WMT) by 3.7B USD. The top two companies maintain a significant revenue gap of 303.7B USD over the third-ranked UnitedHealth Group Incorporated (UNH).
row1.net_income: Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) leads the pack with a net income of $132.2B, a significant gap of $30.1B from NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA) in second place. GOOGL's dominance in the top spot is evident with a substantial lead over the next closest competitor.
row1.total_assets: JPMorgan Chase & Co. (JPM) maintains its position as the largest company by Total Assets, with a significant lead of approximately $1T USD over Bank of America Corporation (BAC). The gap between JPM and the third-largest company, Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN), is even more substantial at around $1.58T USD.
row1.total_equity: Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) leads the pack with a Total Equity of $415.3B USD, a significant gap of $23.2B USD above Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) at $411.1B USD.
row1.total_liabilities: JPMorgan Chase & Co. (JPM) leads the pack with Total Liabilities of 4.1T USD, a significant gap of 1T USD from Bank of America Corporation (BAC) in second place. This substantial gap underscores JPM's larger balance sheet.
row1.cash_and_equivalents: JPMorgan Chase & Co. (JPM) leads the list with 343.3B USD in Cash And Equivalents, a significant gap from Bank of America Corporation (BAC) at 239.3B USD. This 104B USD difference highlights the substantial liquidity advantage JPM holds over its peers.
row1.gross_profit: Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) leads the pack with a gross profit of $360.5B USD, exceeding the next closest competitor by $120.2B USD. This significant gap underscores Amazon's substantial market advantage in terms of profitability.
row1.operating_income_ebit: AAPL leads the pack with a record $133.1B Operating Income Ebit, a $53.7B gap from second-place NVDA. Apple's dominance in technology drives this significant margin.

row2_sector.revenue: Consumer Cyclical and Technology sectors dominate the revenue landscape, accounting for 43.1% of the total with 976.4B USD and 937.6B USD respectively. This concentration highlights the significant influence of these sectors on the overall performance.
row2_sector.total_assets: Financial Services sector dominates the landscape with 64.1% of total assets, nearly 8 times larger than the next closest sector, Technology. This significant concentration suggests a high reliance on the Financial Services sector's performance.
row2_sector.net_income: Technology sector dominates the net income landscape with 39.2% share, nearly doubling the next largest sector, Communication Services, at 24.8%. The Technology sector's significant lead suggests a high level of concentration in this metric.

row2_bubble: NVDA's 55.6% net margin significantly outpaces UNH's 2.7%, indicating stark differences in profitability efficiency. This disparity suggests NVDA's business model is more effective at generating net income from revenue compared to UNH.
row3_heatmap: Morgan Stanley (MA) stands out with a remarkable 193.5% return on equity, indicating exceptional profitability. However, JPMorgan Chase (JPM) takes on significant leverage risk with a debt-to-equity ratio of 11.21x, potentially limiting its long-term growth prospects.
row4_market_trends: Google (GOOGL) led the pack with a 94.3% 12-month cumulative return, significantly outperforming the market. In contrast, Netflix (NFLX) struggled the most with a -40.5% return, highlighting a notable divergence in growth trends. Market sentiment remains mixed with varying levels of investor enthusiasm.
